content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 5, 2004

Charles Wachob, City Attorney
City of Auburn
1225 Lincoln Way
Auburn, CA 95603

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-04-169

Dear Mr. Wachob:

This letter addresses your request for written advice on the clarification of an advisory letter issued by Mayor Cheryl Maki on the provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹ After a careful review of the facts you have submitted, we decline to provide written advice at this time. Your questions involve analysis of facts which have already occurred, such that issuing written advice at this time would relate to past conduct, a circumstance prohibited by regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) and (c)(4)(A), copy enclosed.

Should you have any additional questions on this matter, please contact the Commission at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel

Enclosure
JWW:jg

¹ Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18996, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.conclusion: After a careful review of the facts you have submitted, we decline to provide written advice at this time. Your questions involve analysis of facts which have already occurred, such that issuing written advice at this time would relate to past conduct, a circumstance prohibited by regulation 18329(b)(5)(A) and (c)(1)(A).

Your questions involve analysis of facts which have already occurred, such that issuing written advice at this time would relate to past conduct, a circumstance prohibited by regulation 18329(b)(5)(A) and (c)(1)(A).

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined a request from the City of Auburn's City Attorney for written advice clarifying a prior advisory letter to Mayor Cheryl Maki regarding the Political Reform Act, citing prohibition against issuing advice on past conduct.
